slides.title: Nature’s Power
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 6 English, Unit 7: Nature’s Power. Welcome to Unit 7: Nature’s Power! In this unit, you will explore the incredible and sometimes dangerous forces of the natural world. You will learn about natural disasters such as cyclones, earthquakes, floods, droughts, and volcanic eruptions. You will discover how communities in East Africa and around the world prepare for these events, respond when they strike, and rebuild afterwards. Along the way, you will build a strong set of vocabulary words related to planning, science, government, and community action. You will also study an important grammar topic — how to use the Past Simple and Past Continuous tenses together, joined by connecting words like while, when, and until — to describe events that happened at the same time in the past. These... In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Use and understand key vocabulary about natural forces, science, planning, government, and community
Read and discuss a text about natural disasters and how communities respond to them
Identify the Past Simple tense (for completed actions) and the Past Continuous tense (for actions that were happening when something else occurred)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to use and understand key vocabulary about natural forces, science, planning, government, and community. you will also read and discuss a text about natural disasters and how communities respond to them. you will also identify the past simple tense (for completed actions) and the past continuous tense (for actions that were happening when something else occurred). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: conduction, pollination, stratosphere, excavation, mechanization, illustration. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Nature’s Power: source text 1 and Nature’s Power: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Past Simple vs Past Continuous
bullets: What it means and why it matters The Past Simple describes actions that are finished. The Past Continuous describes actions that were happening — still in...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Past Simple vs Past Continuous. What it means and why it matters The Past Simple describes actions that are finished. The Past Continuous describes actions that were happening — still in progress — at a certain moment in the past. When we use them together, we can tell vivid stories about events that happened at the same time or interrupted each other. This is especially useful when describing natural disasters, emergencies, or any event where many things were happening at once. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Vocabulary Expert Interview. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
